_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize batch: 4 FL Supreme Court justices (federal tier) + 1 GA state senator. Judges scored ONLY on cells with direct personal/judicial evidence; constitutional rulings are NOT read as personhood/policy statements (hostile-reader discipline). Watson scored on documented GA legislative record; guns nulled (a 2A group flagged him as a non-co-sponsor of constitutional carry - not scored high).

profile.background: Appointed to the Florida Supreme Court May 2020 by Gov. DeSantis; former federal prosecutor (S.D. Fla.). Federalist Society member; self-described textualist/originalist. Retained Nov 2022 with 63.7%; next retention 2028. Unanimously elected by his colleagues as the court's next Chief Justice, term beginning July 1, 2026.

john-d-couriel@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: judicial record only. Joined the 6-1 majority in Planned Parenthood v. State of Florida (SC2022-1050, Apr 1 2024) holding the FL Constitution's privacy clause does NOT protect abortion, upholding the 15-week/6-week ban. Also joined the 4-3 majority that allowed the Amendment 4 abortion-rights initiative onto the 2024 ballot (a ballot-access/textualist ruling, NOT a pro-abortion position). Joined the 5-1 majority (July 2025) upholding the DeSantis-backed congressional map. Federalist Society member; textualist. No personal/judicial statement found affirming personhood-at-conception, on marriage/sexuality, religious liberty, or the Second Amendment - those cells null (constitutional rulings are not read as personhood/policy affirmations).

0.note: Joined the majority holding the FL privacy clause doesn't protect abortion, but made no personal statement affirming personhood-at-conception; a constitutional ruling is not a personhood affirmation - null, not inferred.

profile.background: Appointed to the Florida Supreme Court 2009 by Gov. Crist; Chief Justice 2014-2018; first Cuban-American FL Chief Justice. The court's most moderate member and frequent lone dissenter against the post-DeSantis conservative majority. Retained Nov 2022 with 62.3% (lowest of the five on that ballot). Born 1952; subject to FL's mandatory judicial retirement at age 75 (~Oct 2027).

jorge-labarga@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: documented progressive/moderate judicial record. SOLE DISSENTER in Planned Parenthood v. State (SC2022-1050, Apr 1 2024), writing that 'the right of privacy includes the right to an abortion' and lamenting the majority's removal of abortion from constitutional privacy protection - a documented defense of abortion as a constitutional right (contrary to the pro-life position). Sole dissenter (July 2025) against the DeSantis congressional map on minority-representation grounds. Lone dissenter in State v. Poole (2020) which permitted non-unanimous death sentences. His Fried v. State (2023) dissent was on separation-of-powers/local-control grounds and is NOT scored as an anti-2A position (per evidence). No documented positions on marriage, religious liberty, or other rubric categories - null.

0.note: Sole dissenter in Planned Parenthood v. State (2024); wrote 'the right of privacy includes the right to an abortion' - a documented defense of abortion as a constitutional right, contrary to affirming personhood-at-conception.
His Fried v. State (2023) dissent was on separation-of-powers/local-control grounds, not a Second Amendment position - null, not inferred.

profile.background: Appointed to the Florida Supreme Court May 2023 by Gov. DeSantis (former Chief Judge, FL Sixth District Court of Appeal). Federalist Society member; textualist/originalist. Retained Nov 2024 with 62.3%; current term ends Jan 2031 (next retention 2030).

meredith-l-sasso@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: judicial record only. Joined the 6-1 majority in Planned Parenthood v. State (SC2022-1050, Apr 1 2024) holding the FL privacy clause doesn't protect abortion; wrote a separate concurrence on provider standing. Dissented (4-3) from allowing the Amendment 4 abortion-rights initiative onto the ballot, on ballot-summary/truth-in-packaging grounds (a procedural/ballot-integrity rationale, not an explicit personhood statement). Joined the majority upholding the DeSantis congressional map. Federalist Society member; textualist. No personal statement affirming personhood-at-conception, on marriage/sexuality, religious liberty, or the Second Amendment - those cells null (rulings not read as personhood/policy affirmations).

0.note: Joined the majority finding no constitutional abortion right and dissented from the abortion-rights ballot measure on truth-in-packaging grounds, but made no explicit personhood-at-conception statement - null, not inferred.

profile.background: Appointed to the Florida Supreme Court 2022 by Gov. DeSantis; first Jamaican-born FL justice. Federalist Society member; textualist/originalist. Authored a sanctity-of-life dissent in the 2024 Amendment 4 case. Retained Nov 2024 with 63.1%; current term ends Jan 2031 (next retention 2030).

renatha-francis@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: judicial record. Joined the 6-1 majority in Planned Parenthood v. State (2024) finding no constitutional abortion right. DISSENTED (4-3) from placing the Amendment 4 abortion-rights initiative on the ballot and authored a separate opinion with explicit right-to-life language: 'One must recognize the unborn's competing right to life and the State's moral duty to protect that life,' and 'The exercise of a right to an abortion literally results in a devastating infringement on the right of another person: the right to live' - a documented affirmation of the personhood/right-to-life of the unborn (sanctity_of_life q0 TRUE). Joined the majority upholding the DeSantis congressional map. Federalist Society member. Did NOT participate in the Fried gun-preemption case. No documented positions on marriage, religious liberty, or other categories - null.

0.note: Authored an Amendment 4 dissent affirming 'the unborn's competing right to life and the State's moral duty to protect that life' and that abortion 'infringes on the right of another person: the right to live' - a direct, cited affirmation of personhood/right-to-life.

profile.background: Republican Georgia State Senator, District 1 (Savannah/Chatham area); in office since 2015 (GA House 2011-2015). Physician (geriatrician) practicing in Savannah since 1988; chairs the Senate Health & Human Services Committee since 2019. Won re-election Nov 2024; seeking re-election in the 2026 cycle.

ben-watson@GA.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: documented GA legislative record. LIFE: voted YES on HB 481 LIFE Act (2019, 6-week ban), sponsored SB 456 (2022), publicly affirmed 'life begins at conception'; Georgia Life Alliance Pro-Life Certified 2024. GENDER: helped write/sponsor SB 140 (2023) banning hormone therapy/surgery for transgender minors; opposes transgender athletes by gender identity. SCHOOL CHOICE: primary co-sponsor of SB 233 Georgia Promise Scholarship (vouchers); co-sponsored SB 390 (library/ALA materials); supports barring sex-ed before 6th grade and parental notification of library checkouts. RELIGIOUS LIBERTY: supports a Georgia RFRA. ELECTIONS: supported SB 202 (2021 Election Integrity Act - voter ID + ban on private election funding). CRIME: supported SB 63 (anti-bail-reform) and SB 92 (prosecutor-oversight commission). FEDERAL OVERREACH: backed barring COVID-19 vaccine mandates in Georgia. IMMIGRATION: supports defunding non-cooperative (sanctuary) police departments. GUNS: Georgia Gun Owners (GA2A) publicly flagged him in Jan 2022 as one of six Republicans who had NOT co-sponsored constitutional carry, and no NRA/2A rating was found - his Second Amendment cells are NULL (not scored high) pending a confirmed roll-call.

0.note: Publicly stated 'I believe that life begins at conception'; voted YES on HB 481 LIFE Act (2019).

1.note: Supports restrictions (heartbeat ban), but no evidence of advocating abortion abolition specifically - null per the cell's 'not merely restrictions' standard.

2.note: No evidence on embryonic stem-cell/IVF/chimeric research.

4.note: Georgia Life Alliance Pro-Life Certified 2024; no abortion-industry funding found.

0.note: No documented statement affirming one-man-one-woman marriage.

1.note: No documented vote/statement on same-sex marriage law.

2.note: Helped write/sponsored SB 140 (2023) banning hormone therapy/surgery for transgender minors; defended it publicly.

3.note: No evidence on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Opposes transgender athletes competing by gender identity; SB 140 restricts gender-ideology medical interventions for minors.

0.note: Primary co-sponsor of SB 233 Georgia Promise Scholarship (school-choice vouchers); authored a column promoting it.
